title: Regularized Second LightGBM Expert
summary: Add one diversity-focused LightGBM variant with smaller leaves, stronger regularization, and a different seed to the existing fold-wise ensemble.
feature_strategy: Keep all current source-derived photometric, sky, redshift, categorical, frequency, rank, and SDSS17 auxiliary features unchanged. This hypothesis changes only model-panel diversity, not the feature representation.
baseline_model_panel: Use the current CatBoost, XGBoost, primary LightGBM, plus one additional LightGBM regularized expert with num_leaves=63, min_child_samples=85, subsample=0.80, colsample_bytree=0.75, reg_alpha=0.10, reg_lambda=2.50, random_state=2026. Extend the simplex blend search to four model families while keeping fold-wise blend and calibration.
model_panel_rationale: A previous shallow XGBoost variant did not help, but LightGBM is the strongest standalone learner; a more regularized LightGBM may make different boundary errors while remaining aligned with the best model family.
validation_strategy: Use identical 5-fold StratifiedKFold competition-row OOF scoring. The new LightGBM variant is trained inside each fold with the same tree_sw sample weights and evaluated only on the competition validation slice; final OOF score uses fold-wise blend and fold-wise temp-offset calibration.
materialization_hint: Start from response.py. Add LGBM_REG_CONFIG and train lightgbm_reg after the primary LightGBM in each fold; store its OOF/test probabilities; include it in model_names, model_available, fold_scores, fold_times, and the common_model_names blend path. Replace the 3-model-specific simplex search with a generic bounded simplex candidate generator so the four-model blend is valid.
expected_signal: If the regularized LightGBM is complementary, fold-specific blend weights should allocate it nonzero mass and the calibrated OOF score should exceed 0.968002. If it is redundant, the blend search should give it near-zero weight with little score change but extra runtime.
risk: This increases runtime and blend-search dimensionality. The generic candidate grid is bounded and joblib-parallelized, but the extra LightGBM expert may still not add enough diversity to justify its cost.
rationale:
Hypothesis 000031: Add one diversity-focused LightGBM variant with smaller leaves, stronger regularization, and a different seed to the existing fold-wise ensemble.
Parent pipeline score reference: 0.968002 OOF balanced accuracy.
This experiment is intentionally atomic so the score movement can be attributed to the named change.
implementation_hint: Start from response.py. Add LGBM_REG_CONFIG and train lightgbm_reg after the primary LightGBM in each fold; store its OOF/test probabilities; include it in model_names, model_available, fold_scores, fold_times, and the common_model_names blend path. Replace the 3-model-specific simplex search with a generic bounded simplex candidate generator so the four-model blend is valid.
expected_effect: If the regularized LightGBM is complementary, fold-specific blend weights should allocate it nonzero mass and the calibrated OOF score should exceed 0.968002. If it is redundant, the blend search should give it near-zero weight with little score change but extra runtime.

parent_cv_reference.note: Use this hypothesis run score, not the parent score, as the empirical result.

autogluon_feature_transfer.source: legacy feature engineering only; model, fold, calibration, and blending logic intentionally omitted
